title: vatica mangachapoi subsp. obtusifolia
intro: Vatica mangachapoi subsp. obtusifolia, also known as the Borneo ironwood, is a subspecies of tree that belongs to the family Dipterocarpaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Vatica mangachapoi subsp. obtusifolia include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various infections, making it a valuable resource in herbal medicine. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of tea, decoction, or tincture to harness its medicinal properties.
